problem_statement: The repository currently mixes authority assets, governance mirrors, workflow eval fixtures, and reading guides across overlapping paths, creating duplicate canonical sources and ambiguous read chains.
boundary_decisions: Promote architecture/blueprints, governance/policies, governance/records, harness/runtime, harness/eval, eval/domains, and docs as the only canonical asset roots for blueprint, policy, record, runtime, workflow eval, domain eval, and guide concerns respectively.
Remove docs/memory and docs/traceability from the default authority chain; keep docs as guide-only and prohibit duplicate tracked mirrors.
dependency_direction: Harness runtime and workflow tooling may reference governance records and blueprints, but authority assets must not depend on docs guide paths.
interface_freeze_points: All repo-local CLIs, tests, quality gates, and runtime artifacts must accept only the new canonical paths after migration.
ownership_lifecycle_constraints: Project-manager owns the reorg flow; architecture freeze constrains path ownership before coding and traceability work updates the synchronized governance views.
nfr_constraints: The repo must keep one canonical path per asset class and pass quality gates without long-term dual-write compatibility.
forbidden_shortcuts: Do not preserve tracked duplicate copies under docs for convenience after the new canonical paths exist.
tradeoffs: A one-time full history rewrite is preferred over stable dual-path compatibility because ambiguous canonical ownership is the root defect.
